items.title: Import prices post surprise gain as costs of goods from China hit highest since 2008
items.summary: Import prices were up 0.3% for the month, as a drop in energy was more than offset by increases elsewhere.

items.title: World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says
items.summary: The latest edition of the Fed's Beige Book indicated that the soccer tournament wasn't necessarily translating to broader economic growth.

items.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline
items.summary: As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to the brief pause in tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: Renewed Hormuz hostilities drive ECB rates rethink amid ‘extremely volatile’ outlook
items.summary: Several consecutive days of strikes exchanged between the U.S. and Iran have cast uncertainty on the European Central Bank's interest rate decision next week.

items.title: China posts slowest quarterly growth since 2022 as investment slumps, fanning stimulus calls
items.summary: That second-quarter growth came below Beijing's full-year growth target range of 4.5% to 5%, the least ambitious goal in decades.

items.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
items.summary: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.

items.title: India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
items.summary: Inflation has risen for the eighth straight month in India, as food and energy prices rise due to the Iran war and deficient rainfall.

items.title: 'Funflation' hits home: Why staying in isn't the cost-saver it used to be
items.summary: Following a wave of price hikes, at-home pastimes like streaming movies or playing video games are pinching pocketbooks.

items.title: China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high
items.summary: Investors increasingly view China's two-speed growth with robust export and tepid domestic demand as a defining long-term feature of the Chinese economy.

items.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
items.summary: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics.

items.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
items.summary: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

items.title: U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%
items.summary: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

items.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
items.summary: A historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

items.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
items.summary: Nonfarm payrolls are projected to post a gain of 115,000, according to the Dow Jones consensus.

items.title: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June, less than expected, ADP reports
items.summary: Companies added slightly fewer workers than forecast, with hiring targeted heavily toward healthcare-related sectors.

items.title: North Sea oil and renewables: The UK’s next PM faces a defining energy policy decision
items.summary: Andy Burnham could face an early test over North Sea oil drilling as energy shocks revive the UK energy security debate.

items.title: Do you really, really love your job? Then you're not alone, according to surprising results from this survey
items.summary: Those surveyed showed a 78.9% rate of workers who "reported feeling positive at the end of their shifts."

items.title: China widens Japan export curbs, targeting drone makers, nuclear firms and defense institutes
items.summary: China blacklisted four of Japan's government defense research institutes and placed dozens more Japanese firms under tightened export restrictions Monday.

items.title: Shipping rebounds in Strait of Hormuz one week after U.S.-Iran deal – but fragile confidence threatens recovery
items.summary: Shipping traffic through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz is recovering one week after the U.S. and Iran signed an interim peace deal.

items.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation plan after first vessel attack under peace deal
items.summary: The pause follows a container ship being struck near the coast of Oman on Thursday, with a U.S. official saying that Iran was responsible for the attack.

items.title: Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows
items.summary: The personal consumption expenditures price index was expected to show a 4.1% annual increase.

items.title: Trump pledges rapid U.S. response for Venezuela after historic earthquakes kill hundreds
items.summary: The swift U.S. offer of assistance reflects a degree of diplomatic realignment between the Trump administration and the Venezuelan interim government.

items.title: Iran warns ships it's 'unacceptable and dangerous' to transit the Strait of Hormuz without their approval
items.summary: The stern warning underscores Tehran's resolve to retain control over the Strait of Hormuz and to resist transits that bypass its authorization.

items.title: Factory job cuts in June neared financial crisis and Covid levels, S&P says
items.summary: Though the firm's manufacturing index ran better than expected for June, it came largely from an inventory rebuild and despite sharp job cuts.

items.title: U.S. issues sweeping Iran oil sanctions waivers, unlocking billions in revenue for Tehran
items.summary: The U.S. issued a sweeping rollback of sanctions on Iranian oil, offering Tehran an economic lifeline as negotiations toward a peace deal proceed.

items.title: Struggling to find a job? Try looking in Nevada
items.summary: The sparsely populated state known for its world-class casinos and dry climate has been a hiring hot spot.

items.title: Hormuz relief may not ease the economic toll that's already 'baked in,' analysts warn
items.summary: Early signs of reopening of the Strait of Hormuz have lifted the most acute threat to global energy supplies but economic damages from the war will take months to unwind.

items.title: Trump claims Iran deal is 'unconditional surrender,' says his power has 'no limits': Axios
items.summary: Trump said in an interview with Axios on Thursday evening stateside that he negotiated the deal to prevent the conflict from triggering a global depression.

items.title: Bank of England holds interest rates at 3.75% amid Iran war peace prospects
items.summary: The Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee voted 7-2 to keep rates on hold.

items.title: Here are the five big takeaways from Kevin Warsh's first meeting as Fed chairman
items.summary: The Federal Reserve and Chairman Kevin Warsh on Wednesday followed the script on interest rates closely.
